In February {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 42), the following two shrines were merged into the main hall.Shrine noticeboard "Kuwabara Daimyojin" (installed by {{ill|lt=Ishige Town|Q11584779|WD=Q11584779}} and Ishige Town Tourism and Products Association) * Hie Shrine * Satahiko Shrine '''Ranks''' * Jogan 17 (875), December 5 - Senior Sixth Rank Upper''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku,'' Jogan 17, 12th month, 5th day (875), list of promotions. * {{ill|lt=Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shotoku}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1712|Q6871|WD=Q6871}}) - Senior First Rank (as Katori-sha) * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}) - Gosha"National Comprehensive Survey of Shrine Rituals and Festivals" (Jinja Honcho, Heisei 7) * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048}}), May 13 - designated shrine for offering sacred food and cloth File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Torii.jpg|Torii File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Hengaku.jpg|Haiden plaque File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Honden.jpg|Main hall === Enshrined deities === '''Principal deities''' * {{ill|lt=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kumahito|1=UNKNOWN}} '''Associated deities''' * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Precinct shrines === Enshrined at the back of the approach after passing the torii. * Katori-sha Enshrined on the right side facing the main shrine buildings. * Osugi-sha * Yonshagu Enshrined on the left side facing the main shrine buildings. Among the stone shrines are two whose shrine names are not clearly marked. * Koshin-sha * Inari-sha, Hie-sha * Tenjin-sha, Yasaka-sha, Hachiman-sha, Atago-sha File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Katori-sha.jpg|Katori-sha File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Yonshakyu.jpg|Osugi-sha and Yonshagu File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Koushin-sha.jpg|Koshin-sha File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Sarcophagus.jpg|Group of precinct shrines File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Sarcophagus1.jpg|Left side of precinct shrine group File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Sarcophagus2.jpg|Right side of precinct shrine group === Events === * Grand annual festival (November 11) === Access === * 45 minutes on foot (3.5 km) from {{ill|lt=Ishige Station|Q6080189|WD=Q6080189}} on {{ill|lt=Kanto Railway|Q845943|WD=Q845943}} === Footnotes === {{Reflist}}{{神道 横}}{{茨城県の神社一覧目次}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くわはらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuwabara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuwabara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:茨城県の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:岡田郡]] [[Category:茨城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:常総市の建築物]] [[Category:常総市の歴史]][[Category:qqq]]

== Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kuwabara Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi).jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|WD=Q83273}}{{ill|lt=Joso|Q851149|WD=Q851149}}Kunou 1186 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375}}
Ame-no-Kumahito |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Hoki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}} 3 ({{ill|lt=772|Q30729|WD=Q30729}}) |宮司 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = Katori Daimyojin |札所等 = |例祭 = November 11 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Kuwabara Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kuwabara Shrine''' (くわばらじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Kinugawa River|Q1042040|WD=Q1042040}} in {{ill|lt=Joso|Q851149|WD=Q851149}}, {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|WD=Q83273}}. Its two principal deities are {{ill|lt=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375}} and Ame-no-Kumahito. It is a shikinaisha of Okada District, {{ill|lt=Shimosa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695}}, formerly ranked as a gosha. It was the sōsha (general tutelary shrine) of the former {{ill|lt=Okada District|Q11473723|WD=Q11473723}} and {{ill|lt=Toyota District (Ibaraki)|Q11634160|WD=Q11634160|lt=Toyota District}}. === Origin === According to tradition, in March {{ill|lt=772|Q30729|WD=Q30729}} ({{ill|lt=Hoki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}} 3), {{ill|lt=Kuwabara no O|Q27920588|WD=Q27920588}}, provincial governor of {{ill|lt=Shimosa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695}} and grandson of {{ill|lt=Prince Shiki|Q11072254|WD=Q11072254}} (seventh son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802}}), enshrined his ancestors "Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto" and "Ame-no-Kumahito-no-Mikoto" as guardian deities on a plateau called Motoyashiki on the west bank of the Kinugawa. Motoyashiki had a large settlement since ancient times and also had the 10-meter keyhole tumulus "Higashiyamazuka," now lost due to modern levee works. During the Shotai era (898-901), Taira no Yoshimasa placed the Shimosa provincial office at Motoyashiki where this shrine stood and made it a political base. {{ill|lt=Taira no Masakado|Q1207652|WD=Q1207652}} also revered this shrine while using Motoyashiki as a base with his father {{ill|lt=Taira no Yoshimasa|Q11483523|WD=Q11483523}}.Shrine-front stone monument "Origin of Kuwabara Shrine" (installed by Ishige Town Mayor, November 1988) Because residents moved west from Motoyashiki due to flooding and other disasters, the shrine was also relocated to the present site in {{ill|lt=1678|Q7634|WD=Q7634}} ({{ill|lt=Enpo|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735}} 6). In {{ill|lt=1712|Q6871|WD=Q6871}} ({{ill|lt=Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shotoku}} 2), it was raised to Senior First Rank under the shrine name "Katori-sha." In {{ill|lt=1719|Q6902|WD=Q6902}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}} 4), when rebuilding the main hall, a ridge tag from {{ill|lt=1625|Q6826|WD=Q6826}} ({{ill|lt=Kanei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913}} 2) reading "one shrine building established at Kuwabara Daimyojin, Kunou, Shimosa Province, Kanto, Japan" was discovered. In {{ill|lt=1758|Q7639|WD=Q7639}} ({{ill|lt=Horeki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397}} 8), the temple-and-shrine magistrate recognized it as the Engishiki shrine "Kuwabara Shrine," and from then the name reverted to "Kuwabara Shrine." In February {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 42), the following two shrines were merged into the main hall.Shrine noticeboard "Kuwabara Daimyojin" (installed by {{ill|lt=Ishige Town|Q11584779|WD=Q11584779}} and Ishige Town Tourism and Products Association) * Hie Shrine * Satahiko Shrine '''Ranks''' * Jogan 17 (875), December 5 - Senior Sixth Rank Upper''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku,'' Jogan 17, 12th month, 5th day (875), list of promotions. * {{ill|lt=Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shotoku}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1712|Q6871|WD=Q6871}}) - Senior First Rank (as Katori-sha) * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}) - Gosha"National Comprehensive Survey of Shrine Rituals and Festivals" (Jinja Honcho, Heisei 7) * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048}}), May 13 - designated shrine for offering sacred food and cloth File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Torii.jpg|Torii File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Hengaku.jpg|Haiden plaque File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Honden.jpg|Main hall === Enshrined deities === '''Principal deities''' * {{ill|lt=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kumahito|1=UNKNOWN}} '''Associated deities''' * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Precinct shrines === Enshrined at the back of the approach after passing the torii. * Katori-sha Enshrined on the right side facing the main shrine buildings. * Osugi-sha * Yonshagu Enshrined on the left side facing the main shrine buildings. Among the stone shrines are two whose shrine names are not clearly marked. * Koshin-sha * Inari-sha, Hie-sha * Tenjin-sha, Yasaka-sha, Hachiman-sha, Atago-sha File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Katori-sha.jpg|Katori-sha File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Yonshakyu.jpg|Osugi-sha and Yonshagu File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Koushin-sha.jpg|Koshin-sha File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Sarcophagus.jpg|Group of precinct shrines File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Sarcophagus1.jpg|Left side of precinct shrine group File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Sarcophagus2.jpg|Right side of precinct shrine group === Events === * Grand annual festival (November 11) === Access === * 45 minutes on foot (3.5 km) from {{ill|lt=Ishige Station|Q6080189|WD=Q6080189}} on {{ill|lt=Kanto Railway|Q845943|WD=Q845943}} === Footnotes === {{Reflist}}{{神道 横}}{{茨城県の神社一覧目次}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くわはらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuwabara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuwabara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:茨城県の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:岡田郡]] [[Category:茨城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:常総市の建築物]] [[Category:常総市の歴史]][[Category:qqq]]

Its two principal deities are {{ill|lt=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375}} and Ame-no-Kumahito. It is a shikinaisha of Okada District, {{ill|lt=Shimosa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695}}, formerly ranked as a gosha. It was the sōsha (general tutelary shrine) of the former {{ill|lt=Okada District|Q11473723|WD=Q11473723}} and {{ill|lt=Toyota District (Ibaraki)|Q11634160|WD=Q11634160|lt=Toyota District}}. === Origin === According to tradition, in March {{ill|lt=772|Q30729|WD=Q30729}} ({{ill|lt=Hoki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}} 3), {{ill|lt=Kuwabara no O|Q27920588|WD=Q27920588}}, provincial governor of {{ill|lt=Shimosa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695}} and grandson of {{ill|lt=Prince Shiki|Q11072254|WD=Q11072254}} (seventh son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802}}), enshrined his ancestors "Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto" and "Ame-no-Kumahito-no-Mikoto" as guardian deities on a plateau called Motoyashiki on the west bank of the Kinugawa. Motoyashiki had a large settlement since ancient times and also had the 10-meter keyhole tumulus "Higashiyamazuka," now lost due to modern levee works. During the Shotai era (898-901), Taira no Yoshimasa placed the Shimosa provincial office at Motoyashiki where this shrine stood and made it a political base. {{ill|lt=Taira no Masakado|Q1207652|WD=Q1207652}} also revered this shrine while using Motoyashiki as a base with his father {{ill|lt=Taira no Yoshimasa|Q11483523|WD=Q11483523}}.Shrine-front stone monument "Origin of Kuwabara Shrine" (installed by Ishige Town Mayor, November 1988) Because residents moved west from Motoyashiki due to flooding and other disasters, the shrine was also relocated to the present site in {{ill|lt=1678|Q7634|WD=Q7634}} ({{ill|lt=Enpo|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735}} 6). In {{ill|lt=1712|Q6871|WD=Q6871}} ({{ill|lt=Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shotoku}} 2), it was raised to Senior First Rank under the shrine name "Katori-sha." In {{ill|lt=1719|Q6902|WD=Q6902}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}} 4), when rebuilding the main hall, a ridge tag from {{ill|lt=1625|Q6826|WD=Q6826}} ({{ill|lt=Kanei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913}} 2) reading "one shrine building established at Kuwabara Daimyojin, Kunou, Shimosa Province, Kanto, Japan" was discovered. In {{ill|lt=1758|Q7639|WD=Q7639}} ({{ill|lt=Horeki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397}} 8), the temple-and-shrine magistrate recognized it as the Engishiki shrine "Kuwabara Shrine," and from then the name reverted to "Kuwabara Shrine." In February {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 42), the following two shrines were merged into the main hall.Shrine noticeboard "Kuwabara Daimyojin" (installed by {{ill|lt=Ishige Town|Q11584779|WD=Q11584779}} and Ishige Town Tourism and Products Association) * Hie Shrine * Satahiko Shrine '''Ranks''' * Jogan 17 (875), December 5 - Senior Sixth Rank Upper''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku,'' Jogan 17, 12th month, 5th day (875), list of promotions. * {{ill|lt=Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shotoku}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1712|Q6871|WD=Q6871}}) - Senior First Rank (as Katori-sha) * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}) - Gosha"National Comprehensive Survey of Shrine Rituals and Festivals" (Jinja Honcho, Heisei 7) * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048}}), May 13 - designated shrine for offering sacred food and cloth File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Torii.jpg|Torii File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Hengaku.jpg|Haiden plaque File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Honden.jpg|Main hall === Enshrined deities === '''Principal deities''' * {{ill|lt=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kumahito|1=UNKNOWN}} '''Associated deities''' * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Precinct shrines === Enshrined at the back of the approach after passing the torii. * Katori-sha Enshrined on the right side facing the main shrine buildings. * Osugi-sha * Yonshagu Enshrined on the left side facing the main shrine buildings. Among the stone shrines are two whose shrine names are not clearly marked. * Koshin-sha * Inari-sha, Hie-sha * Tenjin-sha, Yasaka-sha, Hachiman-sha, Atago-sha File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Katori-sha.jpg|Katori-sha File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Yonshakyu.jpg|Osugi-sha and Yonshagu File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Koushin-sha.jpg|Koshin-sha File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Sarcophagus.jpg|Group of precinct shrines File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Sarcophagus1.jpg|Left side of precinct shrine group File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Sarcophagus2.jpg|Right side of precinct shrine group === Events === * Grand annual festival (November 11) === Access === * 45 minutes on foot (3.5 km) from {{ill|lt=Ishige Station|Q6080189|WD=Q6080189}} on {{ill|lt=Kanto Railway|Q845943|WD=Q845943}} === Footnotes === {{Reflist}}{{神道 横}}{{茨城県の神社一覧目次}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くわはらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuwabara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuwabara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:茨城県の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:岡田郡]] [[Category:茨城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:常総市の建築物]] [[Category:常総市の歴史]][[Category:qqq]]

== Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kuwabara Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi).jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|WD=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Joso|Q851149|WD=Q851149|1=Jōsō}}Kunou 1186 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}}
Ame-no-Kumahito |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Hoki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} 3 ({{ill|lt=772|Q30729|WD=Q30729|1=772}}) |宮司 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Katori Daimyojin |札所等 = |例祭 = November 11 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Kuwabara Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kuwabara Shrine''' (くわばらじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Kinugawa River|Q1042040|WD=Q1042040|1=Kinugawa River}} in {{ill|lt=Joso|Q851149|WD=Q851149|1=Jōsō}}, {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|WD=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}. Its two principal deities are {{ill|lt=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}} and Ame-no-Kumahito. It is a shikinaisha of Okada District, {{ill|lt=Shimosa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}}, formerly ranked as a gosha. It was the sōsha (general tutelary shrine) of the former {{ill|lt=Okada District|Q11473723|WD=Q11473723|1=Okada district}} and {{ill|lt=Toyota District (Ibaraki)|Q11634160|WD=Q11634160|lt=Toyota District|1=Toyoda district}}. === Origin === According to tradition, in March {{ill|lt=772|Q30729|WD=Q30729|1=772}} ({{ill|lt=Hoki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} 3), {{ill|lt=Kuwabara no O|Q27920588|WD=Q27920588|1=Kuwabara no O}}, provincial governor of {{ill|lt=Shimosa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}} and grandson of {{ill|lt=Prince Shiki|Q11072254|WD=Q11072254|1=Prince Shiki}} (seventh son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}}), enshrined his ancestors "Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto" and "Ame-no-Kumahito-no-Mikoto" as guardian deities on a plateau called Motoyashiki on the west bank of the Kinugawa. Motoyashiki had a large settlement since ancient times and also had the 10-meter keyhole tumulus "Higashiyamazuka," now lost due to modern levee works. During the Shotai era (898-901), Taira no Yoshimasa placed the Shimosa provincial office at Motoyashiki where this shrine stood and made it a political base. {{ill|lt=Taira no Masakado|Q1207652|WD=Q1207652|1=Taira no Masakado}} also revered this shrine while using Motoyashiki as a base with his father {{ill|lt=Taira no Yoshimasa|Q11483523|WD=Q11483523|1=Taira no Yoshimasa}}.Shrine-front stone monument "Origin of Kuwabara Shrine" (installed by Ishige Town Mayor, November 1988) Because residents moved west from Motoyashiki due to flooding and other disasters, the shrine was also relocated to the present site in {{ill|lt=1678|Q7634|WD=Q7634|1=1678}} ({{ill|lt=Enpo|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} 6). In {{ill|lt=1712|Q6871|WD=Q6871|1=1712}} ({{ill|lt=Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shotoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 2), it was raised to Senior First Rank under the shrine name "Katori-sha." In {{ill|lt=1719|Q6902|WD=Q6902|1=1719}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 4), when rebuilding the main hall, a ridge tag from {{ill|lt=1625|Q6826|WD=Q6826|1=1625}} ({{ill|lt=Kanei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 2) reading "one shrine building established at Kuwabara Daimyojin, Kunou, Shimosa Province, Kanto, Japan" was discovered. In {{ill|lt=1758|Q7639|WD=Q7639|1=1758}} ({{ill|lt=Horeki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 8), the temple-and-shrine magistrate recognized it as the Engishiki shrine "Kuwabara Shrine," and from then the name reverted to "Kuwabara Shrine." In February {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057|1=1909}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 42), the following two shrines were merged into the main hall.Shrine noticeboard "Kuwabara Daimyojin" (installed by {{ill|lt=Ishige Town|Q11584779|WD=Q11584779|1=Ishige, Ibaraki}} and Ishige Town Tourism and Products Association) * Hie Shrine * Satahiko Shrine '''Ranks''' * Jogan 17 (875), December 5 - Senior Sixth Rank Upper''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku,'' Jogan 17, 12th month, 5th day (875), list of promotions. * {{ill|lt=Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shotoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1712|Q6871|WD=Q6871|1=1712}}) - Senior First Rank (as Katori-sha) * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}) - Gosha"National Comprehensive Survey of Shrine Rituals and Festivals" (Jinja Honcho, Heisei 7) * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}}), May 13 - designated shrine for offering sacred food and cloth File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Torii.jpg|Torii File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Hengaku.jpg|Haiden plaque File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Honden.jpg|Main hall === Enshrined deities === '''Principal deities''' * {{ill|lt=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kumahito|1=UNKNOWN}} '''Associated deities''' * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Precinct shrines === Enshrined at the back of the approach after passing the torii. * Katori-sha Enshrined on the right side facing the main shrine buildings. * Osugi-sha * Yonshagu Enshrined on the left side facing the main shrine buildings. Among the stone shrines are two whose shrine names are not clearly marked. * Koshin-sha * Inari-sha, Hie-sha * Tenjin-sha, Yasaka-sha, Hachiman-sha, Atago-sha File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Katori-sha.jpg|Katori-sha File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Yonshakyu.jpg|Osugi-sha and Yonshagu File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Koushin-sha.jpg|Koshin-sha File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Sarcophagus.jpg|Group of precinct shrines File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Sarcophagus1.jpg|Left side of precinct shrine group File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Sarcophagus2.jpg|Right side of precinct shrine group === Events === * Grand annual festival (November 11) === Access === * 45 minutes on foot (3.5 km) from {{ill|lt=Ishige Station|Q6080189|WD=Q6080189|1=Ishige Station}} on {{ill|lt=Kanto Railway|Q845943|WD=Q845943|1=Kantō Railway}} === Footnotes === {{Reflist}}{{神道 横}}{{茨城県の神社一覧目次}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くわはらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuwabara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuwabara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:茨城県の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:岡田郡]] [[Category:茨城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:常総市の建築物]] [[Category:常総市の歴史]][[Category:qqq]]

In {{ill|lt=1719|Q6902|WD=Q6902|1=1719}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 4), when rebuilding the main hall, a ridge tag from {{ill|lt=1625|Q6826|WD=Q6826|1=1625}} ({{ill|lt=Kanei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 2) reading "one shrine building established at Kuwabara Daimyojin, Kunou, Shimosa Province, Kanto, Japan" was discovered. In {{ill|lt=1758|Q7639|WD=Q7639|1=1758}} ({{ill|lt=Horeki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 8), the temple-and-shrine magistrate recognized it as the Engishiki shrine "Kuwabara Shrine," and from then the name reverted to "Kuwabara Shrine." In February {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057|1=1909}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 42), the following two shrines were merged into the main hall.Shrine noticeboard "Kuwabara Daimyojin" (installed by {{ill|lt=Ishige Town|Q11584779|WD=Q11584779|1=Ishige, Ibaraki}} and Ishige Town Tourism and Products Association) * Hie Shrine * Satahiko Shrine '''Ranks''' * Jogan 17 (875), December 5 - Senior Sixth Rank Upper''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku,'' Jogan 17, 12th month, 5th day (875), list of promotions. * {{ill|lt=Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shotoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1712|Q6871|WD=Q6871|1=1712}}) - Senior First Rank (as Katori-sha) * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}) - Gosha"National Comprehensive Survey of Shrine Rituals and Festivals" (Jinja Honcho, Heisei 7) * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}}), May 13 - designated shrine for offering sacred food and cloth File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Torii.jpg|Torii File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Hengaku.jpg|Haiden plaque File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Honden.jpg|Main hall === Enshrined deities === '''Principal deities''' * {{ill|lt=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kumahito|1=UNKNOWN}} '''Associated deities''' * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Precinct shrines === Enshrined at the back of the approach after passing the torii. * Katori-sha Enshrined on the right side facing the main shrine buildings. * Osugi-sha * Yonshagu Enshrined on the left side facing the main shrine buildings. Among the stone shrines are two whose shrine names are not clearly marked. * Koshin-sha * Inari-sha, Hie-sha * Tenjin-sha, Yasaka-sha, Hachiman-sha, Atago-sha File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Katori-sha.jpg|Katori-sha File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Yonshakyu.jpg|Osugi-sha and Yonshagu File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Koushin-sha.jpg|Koshin-sha File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Sarcophagus.jpg|Group of precinct shrines File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Sarcophagus1.jpg|Left side of precinct shrine group File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Sarcophagus2.jpg|Right side of precinct shrine group === Events === * Grand annual festival (November 11) === Access === * 45 minutes on foot (3.5 km) from {{ill|lt=Ishige Station|Q6080189|WD=Q6080189|1=Ishige Station}} on {{ill|lt=Kanto Railway|Q845943|WD=Q845943|1=Kantō Railway}} === Footnotes === {{Reflist}}{{神道 横}}{{茨城県の神社一覧目次}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くわはらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuwabara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuwabara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:茨城県の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:岡田郡]] [[Category:茨城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:常総市の建築物]] [[Category:常総市の歴史]]

category (P373) == * Kuwabara-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '茨城県常総市国生1186', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kuwabara-jinja}} [[JA:桑原神社 (常総市)]] {{wikidata link|Q70203929}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kuwabara Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi).jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|WD=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Joso|Q851149|WD=Q851149|1=Jōsō}}Kunou 1186 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}}
Ame-no-Kumahito |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Hoki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} 3 ({{ill|lt=772|Q30729|WD=Q30729|1=772}}) |宮司 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Katori Daimyojin |札所等 = |例祭 = November 11 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Kuwabara Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kuwabara Shrine''' (くわばらじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Kinugawa River|Q1042040|WD=Q1042040|1=Kinugawa River}} in {{ill|lt=Joso|Q851149|WD=Q851149|1=Jōsō}}, {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|WD=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}. Its two principal deities are {{ill|lt=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}} and Ame-no-Kumahito. It is a shikinaisha of Okada District, {{ill|lt=Shimosa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}}, formerly ranked as a gosha. It was the sōsha (general tutelary shrine) of the former {{ill|lt=Okada District|Q11473723|WD=Q11473723|1=Okada district}} and {{ill|lt=Toyota District (Ibaraki)|Q11634160|WD=Q11634160|lt=Toyota District|1=Toyoda district}}. === Origin === According to tradition, in March {{ill|lt=772|Q30729|WD=Q30729|1=772}} ({{ill|lt=Hoki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} 3), {{ill|lt=Kuwabara no O|Q27920588|WD=Q27920588|1=Kuwabara no O}}, provincial governor of {{ill|lt=Shimosa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}} and grandson of {{ill|lt=Prince Shiki|Q11072254|WD=Q11072254|1=Prince Shiki}} (seventh son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}}), enshrined his ancestors "Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto" and "Ame-no-Kumahito-no-Mikoto" as guardian deities on a plateau called Motoyashiki on the west bank of the Kinugawa. Motoyashiki had a large settlement since ancient times and also had the 10-meter keyhole tumulus "Higashiyamazuka," now lost due to modern levee works. During the Shotai era (898-901), Taira no Yoshimasa placed the Shimosa provincial office at Motoyashiki where this shrine stood and made it a political base. {{ill|lt=Taira no Masakado|Q1207652|WD=Q1207652|1=Taira no Masakado}} also revered this shrine while using Motoyashiki as a base with his father {{ill|lt=Taira no Yoshimasa|Q11483523|WD=Q11483523|1=Taira no Yoshimasa}}.Shrine-front stone monument "Origin of Kuwabara Shrine" (installed by Ishige Town Mayor, November 1988) Because residents moved west from Motoyashiki due to flooding and other disasters, the shrine was also relocated to the present site in {{ill|lt=1678|Q7634|WD=Q7634|1=1678}} ({{ill|lt=Enpo|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} 6). In {{ill|lt=1712|Q6871|WD=Q6871|1=1712}} ({{ill|lt=Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shotoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 2), it was raised to Senior First Rank under the shrine name "Katori-sha." In {{ill|lt=1719|Q6902|WD=Q6902|1=1719}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 4), when rebuilding the main hall, a ridge tag from {{ill|lt=1625|Q6826|WD=Q6826|1=1625}} ({{ill|lt=Kanei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 2) reading "one shrine building established at Kuwabara Daimyojin, Kunou, Shimosa Province, Kanto, Japan" was discovered. In {{ill|lt=1758|Q7639|WD=Q7639|1=1758}} ({{ill|lt=Horeki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 8), the temple-and-shrine magistrate recognized it as the Engishiki shrine "Kuwabara Shrine," and from then the name reverted to "Kuwabara Shrine." In February {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057|1=1909}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 42), the following two shrines were merged into the main hall.Shrine noticeboard "Kuwabara Daimyojin" (installed by {{ill|lt=Ishige Town|Q11584779|WD=Q11584779|1=Ishige, Ibaraki}} and Ishige Town Tourism and Products Association) * Hie Shrine * Satahiko Shrine '''Ranks''' * Jogan 17 (875), December 5 - Senior Sixth Rank Upper''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku,'' Jogan 17, 12th month, 5th day (875), list of promotions. * {{ill|lt=Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shotoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1712|Q6871|WD=Q6871|1=1712}}) - Senior First Rank (as Katori-sha) * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}) - Gosha"National Comprehensive Survey of Shrine Rituals and Festivals" (Jinja Honcho, Heisei 7) * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}}), May 13 - designated shrine for offering sacred food and cloth File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Torii.jpg|Torii File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Hengaku.jpg|Haiden plaque File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Honden.jpg|Main hall === Enshrined deities === '''Principal deities''' * {{ill|lt=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kumahito|1=UNKNOWN}} '''Associated deities''' * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Precinct shrines === Enshrined at the back of the approach after passing the torii. * Katori-sha Enshrined on the right side facing the main shrine buildings. * Osugi-sha * Yonshagu Enshrined on the left side facing the main shrine buildings. Among the stone shrines are two whose shrine names are not clearly marked. * Koshin-sha * Inari-sha, Hie-sha * Tenjin-sha, Yasaka-sha, Hachiman-sha, Atago-sha File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Katori-sha.jpg|Katori-sha File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Yonshakyu.jpg|Osugi-sha and Yonshagu File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Koushin-sha.jpg|Koshin-sha File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Sarcophagus.jpg|Group of precinct shrines File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Sarcophagus1.jpg|Left side of precinct shrine group File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Sarcophagus2.jpg|Right side of precinct shrine group === Events === * Grand annual festival (November 11) === Access === * 45 minutes on foot (3.5 km) from {{ill|lt=Ishige Station|Q6080189|WD=Q6080189|1=Ishige Station}} on {{ill|lt=Kanto Railway|Q845943|WD=Q845943|1=Kantō Railway}} === Footnotes === {{Reflist}}{{神道 横}}{{茨城県の神社一覧目次}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuwabara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuwabara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:茨城県の神社]] [[Category:下総国の式内社]] [[Category:岡田郡]] [[Category:茨城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:常総市の建築物]] [[Category:常総市の歴史]]

== Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kuwabara Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi).jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|WD=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Joso|Q851149|WD=Q851149|1=Jōsō}}Kunou 1186 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}}
Ame-no-Kumahito |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Hoki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} 3 ({{ill|lt=772|Q30729|WD=Q30729|1=772}}) |宮司 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Katori Daimyojin |札所等 = |例祭 = November 11 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Kuwabara Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kuwabara Shrine''' (くわばらじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Kinugawa River|Q1042040|WD=Q1042040|1=Kinugawa River}} in {{ill|lt=Joso|Q851149|WD=Q851149|1=Jōsō}}, {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|WD=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}. Its two principal deities are {{ill|lt=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}} and Ame-no-Kumahito. It is a shikinaisha of Okada District, {{ill|lt=Shimosa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}}, formerly ranked as a gosha. It was the sōsha (general tutelary shrine) of the former {{ill|lt=Okada District|Q11473723|WD=Q11473723|1=Okada district}} and {{ill|lt=Toyota District (Ibaraki)|Q11634160|WD=Q11634160|lt=Toyota District|1=Toyoda district}}. === Origin === According to tradition, in March {{ill|lt=772|Q30729|WD=Q30729|1=772}} ({{ill|lt=Hoki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} 3), {{ill|lt=Kuwabara no O|Q27920588|WD=Q27920588|1=Kuwabara no O}}, provincial governor of {{ill|lt=Shimosa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}} and grandson of {{ill|lt=Prince Shiki|Q11072254|WD=Q11072254|1=Prince Shiki}} (seventh son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}}), enshrined his ancestors "Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto" and "Ame-no-Kumahito-no-Mikoto" as guardian deities on a plateau called Motoyashiki on the west bank of the Kinugawa. Motoyashiki had a large settlement since ancient times and also had the 10-meter keyhole tumulus "Higashiyamazuka," now lost due to modern levee works. During the Shotai era (898-901), Taira no Yoshimasa placed the Shimosa provincial office at Motoyashiki where this shrine stood and made it a political base. {{ill|lt=Taira no Masakado|Q1207652|WD=Q1207652|1=Taira no Masakado}} also revered this shrine while using Motoyashiki as a base with his father {{ill|lt=Taira no Yoshimasa|Q11483523|WD=Q11483523|1=Taira no Yoshimasa}}.Shrine-front stone monument "Origin of Kuwabara Shrine" (installed by Ishige Town Mayor, November 1988) Because residents moved west from Motoyashiki due to flooding and other disasters, the shrine was also relocated to the present site in {{ill|lt=1678|Q7634|WD=Q7634|1=1678}} ({{ill|lt=Enpo|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} 6). In {{ill|lt=1712|Q6871|WD=Q6871|1=1712}} ({{ill|lt=Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shotoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 2), it was raised to Senior First Rank under the shrine name "Katori-sha." In {{ill|lt=1719|Q6902|WD=Q6902|1=1719}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 4), when rebuilding the main hall, a ridge tag from {{ill|lt=1625|Q6826|WD=Q6826|1=1625}} ({{ill|lt=Kanei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 2) reading "one shrine building established at Kuwabara Daimyojin, Kunou, Shimosa Province, Kanto, Japan" was discovered. In {{ill|lt=1758|Q7639|WD=Q7639|1=1758}} ({{ill|lt=Horeki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 8), the temple-and-shrine magistrate recognized it as the Engishiki shrine "Kuwabara Shrine," and from then the name reverted to "Kuwabara Shrine." In February {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057|1=1909}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 42), the following two shrines were merged into the main hall.Shrine noticeboard "Kuwabara Daimyojin" (installed by {{ill|lt=Ishige Town|Q11584779|WD=Q11584779|1=Ishige, Ibaraki}} and Ishige Town Tourism and Products Association) * Hie Shrine * Satahiko Shrine '''Ranks''' * Jogan 17 (875), December 5 - Senior Sixth Rank Upper''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku,'' Jogan 17, 12th month, 5th day (875), list of promotions. * {{ill|lt=Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shotoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1712|Q6871|WD=Q6871|1=1712}}) - Senior First Rank (as Katori-sha) * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}) - Gosha"National Comprehensive Survey of Shrine Rituals and Festivals" (Jinja Honcho, Heisei 7) * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}}), May 13 - designated shrine for offering sacred food and cloth File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Torii.jpg|Torii File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Hengaku.jpg|Haiden plaque File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Honden.jpg|Main hall === Enshrined deities === '''Principal deities''' * {{ill|lt=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kumahito|1=UNKNOWN}} '''Associated deities''' * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Precinct shrines === Enshrined at the back of the approach after passing the torii. * Katori-sha Enshrined on the right side facing the main shrine buildings. * Osugi-sha * Yonshagu Enshrined on the left side facing the main shrine buildings. Among the stone shrines are two whose shrine names are not clearly marked. * Koshin-sha * Inari-sha, Hie-sha * Tenjin-sha, Yasaka-sha, Hachiman-sha, Atago-sha File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Katori-sha.jpg|Katori-sha File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Yonshakyu.jpg|Osugi-sha and Yonshagu File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Koushin-sha.jpg|Koshin-sha File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Sarcophagus.jpg|Group of precinct shrines File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Sarcophagus1.jpg|Left side of precinct shrine group File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Sarcophagus2.jpg|Right side of precinct shrine group === Events === * Grand annual festival (November 11) === Access === * 45 minutes on foot (3.5 km) from {{ill|lt=Ishige Station|Q6080189|WD=Q6080189|1=Ishige Station}} on {{ill|lt=Kanto Railway|Q845943|WD=Q845943|1=Kantō Railway}} === Footnotes === {{Reflist}}{{神道 横}}{{茨城県の神社一覧目次}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuwabara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuwabara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:茨城県の神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:岡田郡]] [[Category:茨城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:常総市の建築物]] [[Category:常総市の歴史]]

== Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kuwabara Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi).jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|WD=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Joso|Q851149|WD=Q851149|1=Jōsō}}Kunou 1186 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}}
Ame-no-Kumahito |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Hoki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} 3 ({{ill|lt=772|Q30729|WD=Q30729|1=772}}) |宮司 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Katori Daimyojin |札所等 = |例祭 = November 11 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Kuwabara Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kuwabara Shrine''' (くわばらじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Kinugawa River|Q1042040|WD=Q1042040|1=Kinugawa River}} in {{ill|lt=Joso|Q851149|WD=Q851149|1=Jōsō}}, {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|WD=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}. Its two principal deities are {{ill|lt=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}} and Ame-no-Kumahito. It is a shikinaisha of Okada District, {{ill|lt=Shimosa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}}, formerly ranked as a gosha. It was the sōsha (general tutelary shrine) of the former {{ill|lt=Okada District|Q11473723|WD=Q11473723|1=Okada district}} and {{ill|lt=Toyota District (Ibaraki)|Q11634160|WD=Q11634160|lt=Toyota District|1=Toyoda district}}. === Origin === According to tradition, in March {{ill|lt=772|Q30729|WD=Q30729|1=772}} ({{ill|lt=Hoki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} 3), {{ill|lt=Kuwabara no O|Q27920588|WD=Q27920588|1=Kuwabara no O}}, provincial governor of {{ill|lt=Shimosa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}} and grandson of {{ill|lt=Prince Shiki|Q11072254|WD=Q11072254|1=Prince Shiki}} (seventh son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}}), enshrined his ancestors "Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto" and "Ame-no-Kumahito-no-Mikoto" as guardian deities on a plateau called Motoyashiki on the west bank of the Kinugawa. Motoyashiki had a large settlement since ancient times and also had the 10-meter keyhole tumulus "Higashiyamazuka," now lost due to modern levee works. During the Shotai era (898-901), Taira no Yoshimasa placed the Shimosa provincial office at Motoyashiki where this shrine stood and made it a political base. {{ill|lt=Taira no Masakado|Q1207652|WD=Q1207652|1=Taira no Masakado}} also revered this shrine while using Motoyashiki as a base with his father {{ill|lt=Taira no Yoshimasa|Q11483523|WD=Q11483523|1=Taira no Yoshimasa}}.Shrine-front stone monument "Origin of Kuwabara Shrine" (installed by Ishige Town Mayor, November 1988) Because residents moved west from Motoyashiki due to flooding and other disasters, the shrine was also relocated to the present site in {{ill|lt=1678|Q7634|WD=Q7634|1=1678}} ({{ill|lt=Enpo|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} 6). In {{ill|lt=1712|Q6871|WD=Q6871|1=1712}} ({{ill|lt=Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shotoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 2), it was raised to Senior First Rank under the shrine name "Katori-sha." In {{ill|lt=1719|Q6902|WD=Q6902|1=1719}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 4), when rebuilding the main hall, a ridge tag from {{ill|lt=1625|Q6826|WD=Q6826|1=1625}} ({{ill|lt=Kanei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 2) reading "one shrine building established at Kuwabara Daimyojin, Kunou, Shimosa Province, Kanto, Japan" was discovered. In {{ill|lt=1758|Q7639|WD=Q7639|1=1758}} ({{ill|lt=Horeki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 8), the temple-and-shrine magistrate recognized it as the Engishiki shrine "Kuwabara Shrine," and from then the name reverted to "Kuwabara Shrine." In February {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057|1=1909}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 42), the following two shrines were merged into the main hall.Shrine noticeboard "Kuwabara Daimyojin" (installed by {{ill|lt=Ishige Town|Q11584779|WD=Q11584779|1=Ishige, Ibaraki}} and Ishige Town Tourism and Products Association) * Hie Shrine * Satahiko Shrine '''Ranks''' * Jogan 17 (875), December 5 - Senior Sixth Rank Upper''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku,'' Jogan 17, 12th month, 5th day (875), list of promotions. * {{ill|lt=Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shotoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1712|Q6871|WD=Q6871|1=1712}}) - Senior First Rank (as Katori-sha) * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}) - Gosha"National Comprehensive Survey of Shrine Rituals and Festivals" (Jinja Honcho, Heisei 7) * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}}), May 13 - designated shrine for offering sacred food and cloth File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Torii.jpg|Torii File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Hengaku.jpg|Haiden plaque File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Honden.jpg|Main hall === Enshrined deities === '''Principal deities''' * {{ill|lt=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kumahito|1=UNKNOWN}} '''Associated deities''' * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Precinct shrines === Enshrined at the back of the approach after passing the torii. * Katori-sha Enshrined on the right side facing the main shrine buildings. * Osugi-sha * Yonshagu Enshrined on the left side facing the main shrine buildings. Among the stone shrines are two whose shrine names are not clearly marked. * Koshin-sha * Inari-sha, Hie-sha * Tenjin-sha, Yasaka-sha, Hachiman-sha, Atago-sha File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Katori-sha.jpg|Katori-sha File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Yonshakyu.jpg|Osugi-sha and Yonshagu File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Koushin-sha.jpg|Koshin-sha File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Sarcophagus.jpg|Group of precinct shrines File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Sarcophagus1.jpg|Left side of precinct shrine group File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Sarcophagus2.jpg|Right side of precinct shrine group === Events === * Grand annual festival (November 11) === Access === * 45 minutes on foot (3.5 km) from {{ill|lt=Ishige Station|Q6080189|WD=Q6080189|1=Ishige Station}} on {{ill|lt=Kanto Railway|Q845943|WD=Q845943|1=Kantō Railway}} === Footnotes === {{Reflist}}{{神道 横}}{{茨城県の神社一覧目次}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuwabara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuwabara Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:岡田郡]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:常総市の建築物]] [[Category:常総市の歴史]]

Motoyashiki had a large settlement since ancient times and also had the 10-meter keyhole tumulus "Higashiyamazuka," now lost due to modern levee works. During the Shotai era (898-901), Taira no Yoshimasa placed the Shimosa provincial office at Motoyashiki where this shrine stood and made it a political base. {{ill|lt=Taira no Masakado|Q1207652|WD=Q1207652|1=Taira no Masakado}} also revered this shrine while using Motoyashiki as a base with his father {{ill|lt=Taira no Yoshimasa|Q11483523|WD=Q11483523|1=Taira no Yoshimasa}}.Shrine-front stone monument "Origin of Kuwabara Shrine" (installed by Ishige Town Mayor, November 1988) Because residents moved west from Motoyashiki due to flooding and other disasters, the shrine was also relocated to the present site in {{ill|lt=1678|Q7634|WD=Q7634|1=1678}} ({{ill|lt=Enpo|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} 6). In {{ill|lt=1712|Q6871|WD=Q6871|1=1712}} ({{ill|lt=Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shotoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 2), it was raised to Senior First Rank under the shrine name "Katori-sha." In {{ill|lt=1719|Q6902|WD=Q6902|1=1719}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 4), when rebuilding the main hall, a ridge tag from {{ill|lt=1625|Q6826|WD=Q6826|1=1625}} ({{ill|lt=Kanei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 2) reading "one shrine building established at Kuwabara Daimyojin, Kunou, Shimosa Province, Kanto, Japan" was discovered. In {{ill|lt=1758|Q7639|WD=Q7639|1=1758}} ({{ill|lt=Horeki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 8), the temple-and-shrine magistrate recognized it as the Engishiki shrine "Kuwabara Shrine," and from then the name reverted to "Kuwabara Shrine." In February {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057|1=1909}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 42), the following two shrines were merged into the main hall.Shrine noticeboard "Kuwabara Daimyojin" (installed by {{ill|lt=Ishige Town|Q11584779|WD=Q11584779|1=Ishige, Ibaraki}} and Ishige Town Tourism and Products Association) * Hie Shrine * Satahiko Shrine '''Ranks''' * Jogan 17 (875), December 5 - Senior Sixth Rank Upper''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku,'' Jogan 17, 12th month, 5th day (875), list of promotions. * {{ill|lt=Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shotoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1712|Q6871|WD=Q6871|1=1712}}) - Senior First Rank (as Katori-sha) * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}) - Gosha"National Comprehensive Survey of Shrine Rituals and Festivals" (Jinja Honcho, Heisei 7) * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}}), May 13 - designated shrine for offering sacred food and cloth File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Torii.jpg|Torii File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Hengaku.jpg|Haiden plaque File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Honden.jpg|Main hall === Enshrined deities === '''Principal deities''' * {{ill|lt=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kumahito|1=UNKNOWN}} '''Associated deities''' * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Precinct shrines === Enshrined at the back of the approach after passing the torii. * Katori-sha Enshrined on the right side facing the main shrine buildings. * Osugi-sha * Yonshagu Enshrined on the left side facing the main shrine buildings. Among the stone shrines are two whose shrine names are not clearly marked. * Koshin-sha * Inari-sha, Hie-sha * Tenjin-sha, Yasaka-sha, Hachiman-sha, Atago-sha File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Katori-sha.jpg|Katori-sha File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Yonshakyu.jpg|Osugi-sha and Yonshagu File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Koushin-sha.jpg|Koshin-sha File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Sarcophagus.jpg|Group of precinct shrines File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Sarcophagus1.jpg|Left side of precinct shrine group File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Sarcophagus2.jpg|Right side of precinct shrine group === Events === * Grand annual festival (November 11) === Access === * 45 minutes on foot (3.5 km) from {{ill|lt=Ishige Station|Q6080189|WD=Q6080189|1=Ishige Station}} on {{ill|lt=Kanto Railway|Q845943|WD=Q845943|1=Kantō Railway}} === Footnotes === {{Reflist}}{{神道 横}}{{茨城県の神社一覧目次}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuwabara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuwabara Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:岡田郡]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:常総市の建築物]] [[Category:常総市の歴史]]

== Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kuwabara Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi).jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|qid=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Joso|Q851149|qid=Q851149|1=Jōsō}}Kunou 1186 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|qid=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}}
Ame-no-Kumahito |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Hoki|Q18520676|qid=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} 3 ({{ill|lt=772|Q30729|qid=Q30729|1=772}}) |宮司 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Katori Daimyojin |札所等 = |例祭 = November 11 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Kuwabara Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kuwabara Shrine''' (くわばらじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Kinugawa River|Q1042040|qid=Q1042040|1=Kinugawa River}} in {{ill|lt=Joso|Q851149|qid=Q851149|1=Jōsō}}, {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|qid=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}. Its two principal deities are {{ill|lt=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|qid=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}} and Ame-no-Kumahito. It is a shikinaisha of Okada District, {{ill|lt=Shimosa Province|Q1053695|qid=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}}, formerly ranked as a gosha. It was the sōsha (general tutelary shrine) of the former {{ill|lt=Okada District|Q11473723|qid=Q11473723|1=Okada district}} and {{ill|lt=Toyota District (Ibaraki)|Q11634160|qid=Q11634160|lt=Toyota District|1=Toyoda district}}. === Origin === According to tradition, in March {{ill|lt=772|Q30729|qid=Q30729|1=772}} ({{ill|lt=Hoki|Q18520676|qid=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} 3), {{ill|lt=Kuwabara no O|Q27920588|qid=Q27920588|1=Kuwabara no O}}, provincial governor of {{ill|lt=Shimosa Province|Q1053695|qid=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}} and grandson of {{ill|lt=Prince Shiki|Q11072254|qid=Q11072254|1=Prince Shiki}} (seventh son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|qid=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}}), enshrined his ancestors "Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto" and "Ame-no-Kumahito-no-Mikoto" as guardian deities on a plateau called Motoyashiki on the west bank of the Kinugawa. Motoyashiki had a large settlement since ancient times and also had the 10-meter keyhole tumulus "Higashiyamazuka," now lost due to modern levee works. During the Shotai era (898-901), Taira no Yoshimasa placed the Shimosa provincial office at Motoyashiki where this shrine stood and made it a political base. {{ill|lt=Taira no Masakado|Q1207652|qid=Q1207652|1=Taira no Masakado}} also revered this shrine while using Motoyashiki as a base with his father {{ill|lt=Taira no Yoshimasa|Q11483523|qid=Q11483523|1=Taira no Yoshimasa}}.Shrine-front stone monument "Origin of Kuwabara Shrine" (installed by Ishige Town Mayor, November 1988) Because residents moved west from Motoyashiki due to flooding and other disasters, the shrine was also relocated to the present site in {{ill|lt=1678|Q7634|qid=Q7634|1=1678}} ({{ill|lt=Enpo|Q1145735|qid=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} 6). In {{ill|lt=1712|Q6871|qid=Q6871|1=1712}} ({{ill|lt=Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|qid=Q1193848|lt=Shotoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 2), it was raised to Senior First Rank under the shrine name "Katori-sha." In {{ill|lt=1719|Q6902|qid=Q6902|1=1719}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|qid=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 4), when rebuilding the main hall, a ridge tag from {{ill|lt=1625|Q6826|qid=Q6826|1=1625}} ({{ill|lt=Kanei|Q1192913|qid=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 2) reading "one shrine building established at Kuwabara Daimyojin, Kunou, Shimosa Province, Kanto, Japan" was discovered. In {{ill|lt=1758|Q7639|qid=Q7639|1=1758}} ({{ill|lt=Horeki|Q1194397|qid=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 8), the temple-and-shrine magistrate recognized it as the Engishiki shrine "Kuwabara Shrine," and from then the name reverted to "Kuwabara Shrine." In February {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|qid=Q2057|1=1909}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 42), the following two shrines were merged into the main hall.Shrine noticeboard "Kuwabara Daimyojin" (installed by {{ill|lt=Ishige Town|Q11584779|qid=Q11584779|1=Ishige, Ibaraki}} and Ishige Town Tourism and Products Association) * Hie Shrine * Satahiko Shrine '''Ranks''' * Jogan 17 (875), December 5 - Senior Sixth Rank Upper''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku,'' Jogan 17, 12th month, 5th day (875), list of promotions. * {{ill|lt=Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|qid=Q1193848|lt=Shotoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1712|Q6871|qid=Q6871|1=1712}}) - Senior First Rank (as Katori-sha) * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}}) - Gosha"National Comprehensive Survey of Shrine Rituals and Festivals" (Jinja Honcho, Heisei 7) * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|qid=Q2048|1=1907}}), May 13 - designated shrine for offering sacred food and cloth File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Torii.jpg|Torii File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Hengaku.jpg|Haiden plaque File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Honden.jpg|Main hall === Enshrined deities === '''Principal deities''' * {{ill|lt=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|qid=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kumahito|1=UNKNOWN}} '''Associated deities''' * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|qid=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Precinct shrines === Enshrined at the back of the approach after passing the torii. * Katori-sha Enshrined on the right side facing the main shrine buildings. * Osugi-sha * Yonshagu Enshrined on the left side facing the main shrine buildings. Among the stone shrines are two whose shrine names are not clearly marked. * Koshin-sha * Inari-sha, Hie-sha * Tenjin-sha, Yasaka-sha, Hachiman-sha, Atago-sha File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Katori-sha.jpg|Katori-sha File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Yonshakyu.jpg|Osugi-sha and Yonshagu File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Koushin-sha.jpg|Koshin-sha File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Sarcophagus.jpg|Group of precinct shrines File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Sarcophagus1.jpg|Left side of precinct shrine group File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Sarcophagus2.jpg|Right side of precinct shrine group === Events === * Grand annual festival (November 11) === Access === * 45 minutes on foot (3.5 km) from {{ill|lt=Ishige Station|Q6080189|qid=Q6080189|1=Ishige Station}} on {{ill|lt=Kanto Railway|Q845943|qid=Q845943|1=Kantō Railway}} === Footnotes === {{Reflist}}{{神道 横}}{{茨城県の神社一覧目次}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuwabara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuwabara Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:岡田郡]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:常総市の建築物]] [[Category:常総市の歴史]]

== Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kuwabara Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi).jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|qid=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Joso|Q851149|qid=Q851149|1=Jōsō}}Kunou 1186 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|qid=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}}
Ame-no-Kumahito |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Hoki|Q18520676|qid=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} 3 ({{ill|lt=772|Q30729|qid=Q30729|1=772}}) |宮司 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Katori Daimyojin |札所等 = |例祭 = November 11 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Kuwabara Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kuwabara Shrine''' (くわばらじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Kinugawa River|Q1042040|qid=Q1042040|1=Kinugawa River}} in {{ill|lt=Joso|Q851149|qid=Q851149|1=Jōsō}}, {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|qid=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}. Its two principal deities are {{ill|lt=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|qid=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}} and Ame-no-Kumahito. It is a shikinaisha of Okada District, {{ill|lt=Shimosa Province|Q1053695|qid=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}}, formerly ranked as a gosha. It was the sōsha (general tutelary shrine) of the former {{ill|lt=Okada District|Q11473723|qid=Q11473723|1=Okada district}} and {{ill|lt=Toyota District (Ibaraki)|Q11634160|qid=Q11634160|lt=Toyota District|1=Toyoda district}}. === Origin === According to tradition, in March {{ill|lt=772|Q30729|qid=Q30729|1=772}} ({{ill|lt=Hoki|Q18520676|qid=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} 3), {{ill|lt=Kuwabara no O|Q27920588|qid=Q27920588|1=Kuwabara no O}}, provincial governor of {{ill|lt=Shimosa Province|Q1053695|qid=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}} and grandson of {{ill|lt=Prince Shiki|Q11072254|qid=Q11072254|1=Prince Shiki}} (seventh son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|qid=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}}), enshrined his ancestors "Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto" and "Ame-no-Kumahito-no-Mikoto" as guardian deities on a plateau called Motoyashiki on the west bank of the Kinugawa. Motoyashiki had a large settlement since ancient times and also had the 10-meter keyhole tumulus "Higashiyamazuka," now lost due to modern levee works. During the Shotai era (898-901), Taira no Yoshimasa placed the Shimosa provincial office at Motoyashiki where this shrine stood and made it a political base. {{ill|lt=Taira no Masakado|Q1207652|qid=Q1207652|1=Taira no Masakado}} also revered this shrine while using Motoyashiki as a base with his father {{ill|lt=Taira no Yoshimasa|Q11483523|qid=Q11483523|1=Taira no Yoshimasa}}.Shrine-front stone monument "Origin of Kuwabara Shrine" (installed by Ishige Town Mayor, November 1988) Because residents moved west from Motoyashiki due to flooding and other disasters, the shrine was also relocated to the present site in {{ill|lt=1678|Q7634|qid=Q7634|1=1678}} ({{ill|lt=Enpo|Q1145735|qid=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} 6). In {{ill|lt=1712|Q6871|qid=Q6871|1=1712}} ({{ill|lt=Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|qid=Q1193848|lt=Shotoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 2), it was raised to Senior First Rank under the shrine name "Katori-sha." In {{ill|lt=1719|Q6902|qid=Q6902|1=1719}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|qid=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 4), when rebuilding the main hall, a ridge tag from {{ill|lt=1625|Q6826|qid=Q6826|1=1625}} ({{ill|lt=Kanei|Q1192913|qid=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 2) reading "one shrine building established at Kuwabara Daimyojin, Kunou, Shimosa Province, Kanto, Japan" was discovered. In {{ill|lt=1758|Q7639|qid=Q7639|1=1758}} ({{ill|lt=Horeki|Q1194397|qid=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 8), the temple-and-shrine magistrate recognized it as the Engishiki shrine "Kuwabara Shrine," and from then the name reverted to "Kuwabara Shrine." In February {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|qid=Q2057|1=1909}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 42), the following two shrines were merged into the main hall.Shrine noticeboard "Kuwabara Daimyojin" (installed by {{ill|lt=Ishige Town|Q11584779|qid=Q11584779|1=Ishige, Ibaraki}} and Ishige Town Tourism and Products Association) * Hie Shrine * Satahiko Shrine '''Ranks''' * Jogan 17 (875), December 5 - Senior Sixth Rank Upper''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku,'' Jogan 17, 12th month, 5th day (875), list of promotions. * {{ill|lt=Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|qid=Q1193848|lt=Shotoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1712|Q6871|qid=Q6871|1=1712}}) - Senior First Rank (as Katori-sha) * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}}) - Gosha"National Comprehensive Survey of Shrine Rituals and Festivals" (Jinja Honcho, Heisei 7) * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|qid=Q2048|1=1907}}), May 13 - designated shrine for offering sacred food and cloth File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Torii.jpg|Torii File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Hengaku.jpg|Haiden plaque File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Honden.jpg|Main hall === Enshrined deities === '''Principal deities''' * {{ill|lt=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|qid=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kumahito|1=UNKNOWN}} '''Associated deities''' * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|qid=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Precinct shrines === Enshrined at the back of the approach after passing the torii. * Katori-sha Enshrined on the right side facing the main shrine buildings. * Osugi-sha * Yonshagu Enshrined on the left side facing the main shrine buildings. Among the stone shrines are two whose shrine names are not clearly marked. * Koshin-sha * Inari-sha, Hie-sha * Tenjin-sha, Yasaka-sha, Hachiman-sha, Atago-sha File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Katori-sha.jpg|Katori-sha File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Yonshakyu.jpg|Osugi-sha and Yonshagu File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Koushin-sha.jpg|Koshin-sha File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Sarcophagus.jpg|Group of precinct shrines File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Sarcophagus1.jpg|Left side of precinct shrine group File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Sarcophagus2.jpg|Right side of precinct shrine group === Events === * Grand annual festival (November 11) === Access === * 45 minutes on foot (3.5 km) from {{ill|lt=Ishige Station|Q6080189|qid=Q6080189|1=Ishige Station}} on {{ill|lt=Kanto Railway|Q845943|qid=Q845943|1=Kantō Railway}} === Footnotes === {{Reflist}}{{神道 横}}{{茨城県の神社一覧目次}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuwabara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuwabara Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:岡田郡]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:常総市の建築物]] [[Category:常総市の歴史]]

In {{ill|lt=1758|Q7639|qid=Q7639|1=1758}} ({{ill|lt=Horeki|Q1194397|qid=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 8), the temple-and-shrine magistrate recognized it as the Engishiki shrine "Kuwabara Shrine," and from then the name reverted to "Kuwabara Shrine." In February {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|qid=Q2057|1=1909}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 42), the following two shrines were merged into the main hall.Shrine noticeboard "Kuwabara Daimyojin" (installed by {{ill|lt=Ishige Town|Q11584779|qid=Q11584779|1=Ishige, Ibaraki}} and Ishige Town Tourism and Products Association) * Hie Shrine * Satahiko Shrine '''Ranks''' * Jogan 17 (875), December 5 - Senior Sixth Rank Upper''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku,'' Jogan 17, 12th month, 5th day (875), list of promotions. * {{ill|lt=Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|qid=Q1193848|lt=Shotoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1712|Q6871|qid=Q6871|1=1712}}) - Senior First Rank (as Katori-sha) * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}}) - Gosha"National Comprehensive Survey of Shrine Rituals and Festivals" (Jinja Honcho, Heisei 7) * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|qid=Q2048|1=1907}}), May 13 - designated shrine for offering sacred food and cloth File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Torii.jpg|Torii File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Hengaku.jpg|Haiden plaque File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Honden.jpg|Main hall === Enshrined deities === '''Principal deities''' * {{ill|lt=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|qid=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kumahito|1=UNKNOWN}} '''Associated deities''' * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|qid=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Precinct shrines === Enshrined at the back of the approach after passing the torii. * Katori-sha Enshrined on the right side facing the main shrine buildings. * Osugi-sha * Yonshagu Enshrined on the left side facing the main shrine buildings. Among the stone shrines are two whose shrine names are not clearly marked. * Koshin-sha * Inari-sha, Hie-sha * Tenjin-sha, Yasaka-sha, Hachiman-sha, Atago-sha File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Katori-sha.jpg|Katori-sha File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Yonshakyu.jpg|Osugi-sha and Yonshagu File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Koushin-sha.jpg|Koshin-sha File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Sarcophagus.jpg|Group of precinct shrines File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Sarcophagus1.jpg|Left side of precinct shrine group File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Sarcophagus2.jpg|Right side of precinct shrine group === Events === * Grand annual festival (November 11) === Access === * 45 minutes on foot (3.5 km) from {{ill|lt=Ishige Station|Q6080189|qid=Q6080189|1=Ishige Station}} on {{ill|lt=Kanto Railway|Q845943|qid=Q845943|1=Kantō Railway}} === Footnotes === {{Reflist}}{{神道 横}}{{茨城県の神社一覧目次}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuwabara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuwabara Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:岡田郡]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:常総市の建築物]] [[Category:常総市の歴史]]

link|Q70203929|commons|Category:Kuwabara-jinja|ja|桑原神社 (常総市)}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kuwabara Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi).jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|qid=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Joso|Q851149|qid=Q851149|1=Jōsō}}Kunou 1186 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|qid=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}}
Ame-no-Kumahito |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Hoki|Q18520676|qid=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} 3 ({{ill|lt=772|Q30729|qid=Q30729|1=772}}) |宮司 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Katori Daimyojin |札所等 = |例祭 = November 11 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Kuwabara Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kuwabara Shrine''' (くわばらじんじゃ) is a shrine along the {{ill|lt=Kinugawa River|Q1042040|qid=Q1042040|1=Kinugawa River}} in {{ill|lt=Joso|Q851149|qid=Q851149|1=Jōsō}}, {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|qid=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}. Its two principal deities are {{ill|lt=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|qid=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}} and Ame-no-Kumahito. It is a shikinaisha of Okada District, {{ill|lt=Shimosa Province|Q1053695|qid=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}}, formerly ranked as a gosha. It was the sōsha (general tutelary shrine) of the former {{ill|lt=Okada District|Q11473723|qid=Q11473723|1=Okada district}} and {{ill|lt=Toyota District (Ibaraki)|Q11634160|qid=Q11634160|lt=Toyota District|1=Toyoda district}}. === Origin === According to tradition, in March {{ill|lt=772|Q30729|qid=Q30729|1=772}} ({{ill|lt=Hoki|Q18520676|qid=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} 3), {{ill|lt=Kuwabara no O|Q27920588|qid=Q27920588|1=Kuwabara no O}}, provincial governor of {{ill|lt=Shimosa Province|Q1053695|qid=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}} and grandson of {{ill|lt=Prince Shiki|Q11072254|qid=Q11072254|1=Prince Shiki}} (seventh son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|qid=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}}), enshrined his ancestors "Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto" and "Ame-no-Kumahito-no-Mikoto" as guardian deities on a plateau called Motoyashiki on the west bank of the Kinugawa. Motoyashiki had a large settlement since ancient times and also had the 10-meter keyhole tumulus "Higashiyamazuka," now lost due to modern levee works. During the Shotai era (898-901), Taira no Yoshimasa placed the Shimosa provincial office at Motoyashiki where this shrine stood and made it a political base. {{ill|lt=Taira no Masakado|Q1207652|qid=Q1207652|1=Taira no Masakado}} also revered this shrine while using Motoyashiki as a base with his father {{ill|lt=Taira no Yoshimasa|Q11483523|qid=Q11483523|1=Taira no Yoshimasa}}.Shrine-front stone monument "Origin of Kuwabara Shrine" (installed by Ishige Town Mayor, November 1988) Because residents moved west from Motoyashiki due to flooding and other disasters, the shrine was also relocated to the present site in {{ill|lt=1678|Q7634|qid=Q7634|1=1678}} ({{ill|lt=Enpo|Q1145735|qid=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} 6). In {{ill|lt=1712|Q6871|qid=Q6871|1=1712}} ({{ill|lt=Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|qid=Q1193848|lt=Shotoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 2), it was raised to Senior First Rank under the shrine name "Katori-sha." In {{ill|lt=1719|Q6902|qid=Q6902|1=1719}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|qid=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 4), when rebuilding the main hall, a ridge tag from {{ill|lt=1625|Q6826|qid=Q6826|1=1625}} ({{ill|lt=Kanei|Q1192913|qid=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 2) reading "one shrine building established at Kuwabara Daimyojin, Kunou, Shimosa Province, Kanto, Japan" was discovered. In {{ill|lt=1758|Q7639|qid=Q7639|1=1758}} ({{ill|lt=Horeki|Q1194397|qid=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}} 8), the temple-and-shrine magistrate recognized it as the Engishiki shrine "Kuwabara Shrine," and from then the name reverted to "Kuwabara Shrine." In February {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|qid=Q2057|1=1909}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 42), the following two shrines were merged into the main hall.Shrine noticeboard "Kuwabara Daimyojin" (installed by {{ill|lt=Ishige Town|Q11584779|qid=Q11584779|1=Ishige, Ibaraki}} and Ishige Town Tourism and Products Association) * Hie Shrine * Satahiko Shrine '''Ranks''' * Jogan 17 (875), December 5 - Senior Sixth Rank Upper''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku,'' Jogan 17, 12th month, 5th day (875), list of promotions. * {{ill|lt=Shotoku (Japan)|Q1193848|qid=Q1193848|lt=Shotoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1712|Q6871|qid=Q6871|1=1712}}) - Senior First Rank (as Katori-sha) * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}}) - Gosha"National Comprehensive Survey of Shrine Rituals and Festivals" (Jinja Honcho, Heisei 7) * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|qid=Q2048|1=1907}}), May 13 - designated shrine for offering sacred food and cloth File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Torii.jpg|Torii File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Hengaku.jpg|Haiden plaque File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Honden.jpg|Main hall === Enshrined deities === '''Principal deities''' * {{ill|lt=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|Q11633375|qid=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Kumahito|1=UNKNOWN}} '''Associated deities''' * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|qid=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Precinct shrines === Enshrined at the back of the approach after passing the torii. * Katori-sha Enshrined on the right side facing the main shrine buildings. * Osugi-sha * Yonshagu Enshrined on the left side facing the main shrine buildings. Among the stone shrines are two whose shrine names are not clearly marked. * Koshin-sha * Inari-sha, Hie-sha * Tenjin-sha, Yasaka-sha, Hachiman-sha, Atago-sha File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Katori-sha.jpg|Katori-sha File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Yonshakyu.jpg|Osugi-sha and Yonshagu File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Koushin-sha.jpg|Koshin-sha File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Sarcophagus.jpg|Group of precinct shrines File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Sarcophagus1.jpg|Left side of precinct shrine group File:Kuwabara-jinja(Jousou,Ibaragi) Sarcophagus2.jpg|Right side of precinct shrine group === Events === * Grand annual festival (November 11) === Access === * 45 minutes on foot (3.5 km) from {{ill|lt=Ishige Station|Q6080189|qid=Q6080189|1=Ishige Station}} on {{ill|lt=Kanto Railway|Q845943|qid=Q845943|1=Kantō Railway}} === Footnotes === {{Reflist}}{{神道 横}}{{茨城県の神社一覧目次}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kuwabara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kuwabara Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:岡田郡]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:常総市の建築物]] [[Category:常総市の歴史]]
